title: Cynodon fascicularis
intro: Cynodon dactylon, commonly known as Cynodon fascicularis or Bur grass, is a perennial grass native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Cynodon dactylon include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Cynodon dactylon include infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, which are used to treat various health conditions and skin problems.
taxonomy: Cynodon fascicularis is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is classified within the subfamily Chloridoideae and the tribe Cynodonteae. The genus Cynodon is part of the subfamily, which is further classified under the family Poaceae. Cynodon fascicularis is native to tropical and subtropical regions. Its classification is closely related to other species of Cynodon, a genus known for its widespread distribution across the globe.
